caption: Figure 1 The S-N curves of specimens (▲) BL and (△) BT in the reverse bending fatigue test.
paper_abstract: Optical microscopy indicated that after solution treatment, specimen A was fully austenite, specimen B contained 10% ferrite and 90% austenite and specimen C consisted of 55% ferrite and 45% austenite [5]. The results of the tensile tests are summarized in Table I. Each specimen is represented by two letters. The first letter (A, B or C) indicates the alloy type after solution treatment. The second letter indicates the stress direction, L for longitudinal and T for transverse. The data show that the longitudinal specimen possessed higher tensile strength and lower elongation than the transverse specimen for the same type of alloy. However, the differences are not significant. The fatigue S-N curves of alloys AL and CL coincide with the curves of AT and CT. No differences were observed between the fatigue properties of longitudinal specimens and transverse specimens for alloys A and C. The S-N curves of alloy B are shown in Fig. 1 indicating that the fatigue properties of specimen BL are better than specimen BT. It was observed that in alloy B slip bands only occurred in the austenite phase. No slip bands or fatigue cracks were found within the ferrite phase. The fatigue cracks initiated at slip bands and propagated along a direction roughly normal to the applied stress. If the string arrangement of ferrite phase was parallel to the crack propagation direction as in specimens BT, the cracks propagated across the austenite grains, as shown in Fig. 2. If the string arrangement of the ferrite phase was perpendicular to the crack propagation as in specimens BL, it was observed that the crack propagation was impeded by the ferrite phase, as shown in Fig. 3. In this case, it is believed that the ferrite phase serves as a crack arrester. Because the ferrite grains are fine and soft, the blunting effect at the initial stage of fatigue and the intense work hardening at the later stage may lead to the impediment of crack propagation. Because fatigue crack propagation is impeded by the ferrite phase in specimen BL, this specimen is expected to possess better fatigue resistance than specimen BT, which is in agreement with our experimental results.

caption: Figure 2 In specimen BT the fatigue cracks propagated across the austenite grains.
